metaDescription: Learn how to write and print checks using QuickBooks Online for vendor payments, payroll, or client refunds without manual paperwork.
intro: Whether you're paying a vendor, covering payroll, or issuing a refund, writing checks through QuickBooks Online keeps the transaction tied directly to your books. This guide covers how to write checks in QuickBooks Online step by step, including printing tips. It's aimed at small business owners, freelancers, and finance professionals handling regular check payments.

steps.heading: Access the check-writing feature
steps.body: Navigate to the checks section within QuickBooks Online to start creating a new check.

steps.heading: Enter payee and payment details
steps.body: Select the payee, enter the amount, and choose the correct expense or liability account for the payment.

steps.heading: Review before finalizing
steps.body: Double-check the check details for accuracy, including the date and memo, before saving.

steps.heading: Print the check
steps.body: If issuing a physical check, use QuickBooks' print settings to align it correctly with your check stock.

steps.heading: Track and manage payments
steps.body: Use QuickBooks' reporting to keep tabs on which checks have cleared and which are still outstanding.

faq.question: Do I need special check stock to print checks from QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks-compatible check stock ensures your printed checks align correctly with the software's formatting.

faq.question: Can I write a check without printing it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can record a check transaction electronically without printing if payment was made another way, like handwritten.

faq.question: How do I track which checks haven't cleared the bank yet?
faq.answer: QuickBooks' reconciliation and register views let you see outstanding checks that haven't yet cleared your bank.

faq.question: Is check writing suitable for payroll payments?
faq.answer: Yes, though many businesses also use QuickBooks' dedicated payroll features alongside manual check writing when needed.
